title: Drinking Water Quality Monitoring Gaps In Low- And Middle-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for identifying where drinking water quality monitoring is absent and fecal contamination risk is highest, linking JMP water-quality data, water-point mapping, and environmental risk factors at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: WHO/UNICEF JMP reports 2 billion people drink fecally contaminated water. But water-quality testing is sporadic in most LMICs — fewer than 30 countries report sub-national water-quality data to JMP. Satellite-derived environmental risk factors and water-point mapping can estimate contamination risk, but linking these to monitoring gaps has not been done systematically.

success_metrics.target: Every monitoring-gap estimate links JMP data, water-point maps, and environmental risk with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
